artifact.content_preview:
MEETING NOTES — NGIS PHASE 3 STEERING COMMITTEE
Date: March 8, 2026 | Attendees: Director Okafor, Alice Tran, Bob Morrison, ADM Clarke

Item 5 (raised by ADM Clarke):

Following the recent cyber incident affecting a peer department's primary
data centre, ADM Clarke raised the requirement to add a dedicated disaster
recovery (DR) site to the NGIS Phase 3 scope.

ADM Clarke's request:
"Given the current threat environment and Treasury Board's updated guidance
on GC infrastructure resilience, I wou

extracted_tasks.text: Investigate adding a new disaster recovery (DR) site as part of the Phase 3 consolidation, including identifying a colocation facility (Borden or Miramichi) and ensuring hot-standby replicas.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I would like the project team to investigate adding a new disaster recovery site as part of the Phase 3 consolidation.
Action: Alice Tran to prepare DR site options analysis. Due: March 15.

extracted_tasks.text: Formal scope addition requires a change request, ADM approval, and Treasury Board notification if cost impact exceeds 15%.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Any formal scope addition would require a change request, ADM approval, and Treasury Board notification if the cost impact exceeds 15%.

extracted_tasks.text: Decision: A dedicated disaster recovery (DR) site is essential for business continuity compliance despite being a material scope addition.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I believe it is essential for business continuity compliance.

metadata.date: March 8, 2026 | Attendees: Director Okafor, Alice Tran, Bob Morrison, ADM Clarke

alerts.detected_ask: Adding a new disaster recovery (DR) site (Borden or Miramichi) with hot-standby replicas for all migrated workloads.
alerts.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A; Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A.
alerts.suggested_action: Initiate the formal SSC Project Change Management Process. Perform a detailed Impact Assessment (IA) focusing on the $2.4M budget constraint and the April 30, 2026, Go-Live deadline. Prepare a Treasury Board submission briefing, as this material addition will likely exceed the 15% variance threshold

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",
    "Network performance baseline testing post-migration",
    "Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to Site A",
    "AD schema extension and forest restructuring",
    "Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing",
    "Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD",
    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",
    "Monthly steering committee presentations",
    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming (lift and shift only)",
    "GC-Cloud workload migration (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud)",
    "End-user training and change management beyond project communications",
    "Procurement of new application licenses",
    "Network monitoring tool upgrades"
  ],
  "deliverables": [
    "D-001: Project Management Plan",
    "D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD)",
    "D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package",
    "D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C)",
    "D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan",
    "D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live)",
    "D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report",
    "D-008: Project Closure Report"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget of $2,400,000 inclusive of all fees and taxes",
    "Budget variances exceeding 15% require Treasury Board submission",
    "Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date of June 30, 2026",
    "Compliance with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management",
    "Compliance with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "Compliance with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "Compliance with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "Compliance with Official Languages Act for all client-facing deliverables",
    "Adherence to SSC Project Change Management Process for all scope, schedule, or budget changes"
  ]
}
```

Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office

Application modernization or re-platforming (lift and shift only)
End-user training and change management beyond project communications
deliverables: D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C)
constraints: Total approved budget of $2,400,000 inclusive of all fees and taxes
Budget variances exceeding 15% require Treasury Board submission
Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026
Final project closure date of June 30, 2026

Compliance with Official Languages Act for all client-facing deliverables

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.2,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "Adding a new disaster recovery (DR) site (Borden or Miramichi) with hot-standby replicas for all migrated workloads.",
  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A; Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A.",
  "suggested_action": "Initiate the formal SSC Project Change Management Process. Perform a detailed Impact Assessment (IA) focusing on the $2.4M budget constraint and the April 30, 2026, Go-Live deadline. Prepare a Treasury Board submission briefing, as this material addition will likely exceed the 15% variance threshold.",
  "reasoning": "The request represents a significant 'Scope Expansion.' The current baseline is a consolidation project (moving Sites B/C to Site A), not a high-availability/disaster recovery build-out. Adding a third geographic location and 'hot-standby' infrastructure introduces massive unbudgeted costs for hardware, colocation fees, and data replication configuration, and threatens the April 2026 Go-Live constraint."
}
```
